category_title: Quietly observant students who listen to stories or watch peers solve math problems
minimal_context: The student is sitting at their desk with an adapted book for an independent silent reading activity.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for our independent silent reading activity. Your adapted book is on your desk.

STUDENT: (Sits quietly and watches other students complete their tasks.)

TUTOR: Please begin reading.

STUDENT: (Looks around the classroom, leaving the book untouched.)

TUTOR: You should be reading your book now.

STUDENT: (The adapted book remains closed on the desk while the student stares straight ahead.)

options.A: Provide adapted books that classmates are reading, but at lower readability levels, using large fonts with supporting pictures.
options.B: Provide sequenced instruction in the form of rhymes to help the student read the adapted book during the activity.
options.C: Use a two-pocket folder where the left side holds reading to be completed and the right holds completed reading.
options.D: Explicitly incorporate and promote self-compassion within the content of the independent reading activity throughout the session.
